title: Yorushika Hana ni Bourei Tele overdrive solo refinement
reason: Replace the current low-gain clean-leaning drive structure with a researched medium-overdrive lead tone for a Fender Hybrid II Telecaster into FRFR or headphones, while preserving pick dynamics and tightening modulation and ambience.

research.target: Yorushika - Hana ni Bourei studio-style overdriven guitar solo starting point
facts: A public transcription labels the relevant electric-guitar part as overdriven guitar rather than clean guitar.
Published tempo references place the song around 180 BPM, making the existing 333 ms delay close to a quarter-note repeat.
Public n-buna rig summaries repeatedly associate his broader guitar setup with Fender amplification and BOSS Blues Driver variants, but do not prove the exact studio chain for this song.
The official HOTONE algorithm documentation describes Dr. Blues as a dynamic, warm blues overdrive based on a widely used blues overdrive and its PHAT-modified variant.
The installed Ampero II v1.0.8 catalog exposes Dr. Blues Gain, Tone, and Volume parameters from 0 to 100.
inferences: A moderate Dr. Blues stage into the existing Black Twin is a closer starting point than the current low-gain Prince of Drive setting.
Raising drive saturation and amp input volume moderately should create audible overdrive without turning the preset into a high-gain rock lead.
Bypassing the always-on chorus and reducing delay and reverb density should move the overdriven dry note forward in the mix.
A restrained high cut and stronger amp midrange should reduce Telecaster edge while retaining articulation.
limitations: No isolated master-recording guitar stem was available for direct spectral or gain-envelope comparison.
General artist rig reports are supporting context and are not proof of the exact equipment used on Hana ni Bourei.
The result remains a starting point until compared through the user's actual monitoring level and pickup selection.

sources.title: Hana Ni Bourei Guitar Tab
sources.finding: The electric-guitar part is identified as overdriven guitar.

sources.title: Hana ni Bourei tempo reference
sources.finding: The song is listed at approximately 180 BPM.

sources.title: BOSS BD-2W Blues Driver official product documentation
sources.finding: The Blues Driver family is documented as dynamic overdrive, with the custom voicing adding body and sustain.

sources.title: HOTONE CDCM II effects list
sources.finding: Dr. Blues is documented as a warm, touch-sensitive blues overdrive based on a classic blues overdrive and PHAT-modified version.

sources.title: n-buna equipment summaries
sources.finding: Broader rig summaries report Fender amplification and multiple Blues Driver variants in n-buna's equipment history.

sources.title: Ampero II installed algorithm catalog v1.0.8
sources.finding: All proposed model and parameter names and ranges were checked against the locally installed official editor catalog.
